examples.title: Basic Exception Handling
examples.explanation: The divide function throws runtime_error when divisor is zero. The catch block catches it by reference. Multiple catch blocks allow handling different exception types.

examples.title: Custom Exception Class
examples.description: Creating and using user-defined exception class
examples.explanation: Custom exception class derives from std::exception and overrides what() method. Can be caught by catch(const exception&) due to polymorphism.

examples.title: Exception Rethrowing
examples.explanation: Using throw; without an operand rethrows the currently caught exception. This allows partial handling at one level before propagating to outer handlers.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is call stack depth

examples.title: Function Try Block in Constructor
examples.description: Using function try blocks to catch exceptions from initializer list
examples.explanation: Function try blocks allow catching exceptions from base class constructors and member initializer lists. For constructors, exceptions must be rethrown.

examples.title: Stack Unwinding Demonstration
examples.description: Shows how destructors are called during stack unwinding
examples.explanation: When func2 throws, destructor of t(2) runs, then control returns to func1 where destructor of t(1) runs. This is stack unwinding - local objects are properly destroyed.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is stack depth
